regions.text: More complex changes developed later. A burning candle involves both physical and chemical changes.
(Diagram: A burning candle). Melting wax and turning into gas are physical changes.
Inside the flame, carbon and oxygen create carbon dioxide and water. This is a chemical change. You can see the water by holding a glass over the flame.
chemical changes: when the structure of materials changes into something new. Light and heat are also given out.
